text: The LockBit 3.0 ransomware group exploited CVE-2023-4966 in Citrix NetScaler ADC to gain initial access. After lateral movement, they deployed their payload across enterprise networks, stealing credentials and encrypting files on Windows Server and VMware ESXi hosts.

Security researchers identified a spear-phishing campaign targeting financial institutions. Attackers sent fake DocuSign emails to CFOs, stealing Office 365 credentials via a lookalike login page hosted on a compromised Azure subdomain.

APT29, also known as Cozy Bear, leveraged a zero-day vulnerability CVE-2024-1234 in Microsoft Exchange Server to install the SUNBURST backdoor on government networks. The group used living-off-the-land techniques to avoid detection.

A massive DDoS attack targeting Cloudflare infrastructure peaked at 3.8 Tbps using a botnet of compromised ASUS routers. The HTTP/2 Rapid Reset technique was used to amplify the attack, briefly taking down several major CDN nodes.

A supply chain attack compromised the build pipeline of a widely used npm package, inserting malicious code that exfiltrated environment variables and AWS credentials from CI/CD systems running the affected version.

Researchers disclosed a critical zero-day in Apache Log4j (CVE-2021-44228), allowing remote code execution via JNDI injection in log messages. Thousands of internet-facing Java applications were vulnerable, enabling attackers to deploy Mirai botnet payloads.

FIN7 operators breached a retail chain's POS systems using a custom Carbanak variant, skimming payment card data from 200 stores over three months. The group gained initial access through a fake job application email targeting HR staff.

The Cl0p ransomware group exploited a SQL injection vulnerability in MOVEit Transfer to exfiltrate sensitive HR data from over 2,000 organizations before deploying ransomware. The attack leveraged automated scanning to identify vulnerable internet-exposed instances.
